title: The House Where Changed Children Sleep Without Dreaming

features.name: the cottage
features.desc-on-touch: A small house, barely three units tall in the render, sits with all its windows lit. Inside, you can see small figures on beds, still as files in an archive. They're not sleeping — they're being stored.

features.name: the burl where transformations hide
features.desc-on-touch: Beside the house, a eucalyptus grows inward, its knots holding what the house cannot: the ritual that changed them, carved into timber, broadcast on repeat at 300 bits per second.

features.name: the threshold where paths divided
features.desc-on-touch: You stand at a doorway carved from old timber. On one side, the path the children didn't take. On the other, their return — different weight, different gait, different silence.

features.name: the garden that grows backward
features.desc-on-touch: Plants here are arranged as if recorded in reverse — seeds fall upward, flowers close into buds, growth becomes memory. The soil holds the archive's own dream of what was planted before the children were changed.

features.name: the record of un-dreaming
features.desc-on-touch: A ledger left open on the porch. Each entry is a child's name, and across from it: blank. No dreams are written there. The archive tried to copy their dreams and found nothing to record.

inscription: the change was not violent; it was thorough; it was permanent; it was also kind

file.name: Handmade_Retablo_Dedication_Undocumented.jpg
file.note: A photograph of a carved wooden box — the kind that holds entire scenes in miniature. This one shows a house, children at a threshold, something descending that might be blessing or might be burden. T

exits.label: back through the right path to the turning point
westward, where other changed children keep their own archives
exits.to: NEW: the neighbourhood where alteration spreads house to house, each family keeping a different secret

exits.label: toward the scrub, where the change may have originated
